preamble: The realization of the voiceless uvular stop /q/ as a voiced velar stop [ɡ] is a hallmark of Bedouin-descended and Gulf Arabic dialects. This shift involves both a change in the place of articulation (fronting) and the addition of voicing.

examples.note: Systematic realization of /q/ as [ɡ] in Bedouin speech.

sources: Holes, C. (2004). Modern Arabic: Structures, Functions, and Varieties. Georgetown University Press.
Watson, J. C. (2002). The Phonology and Morphology of Arabic. Oxford University Press.
